title: Radiant Chinese Sweet Potato Stir Fry
description: A mustardy, fluffy, comforting Chinese dish that showcases sweet potatoes and braised scallops in a fragrant soffritto base, ideal for pescatarian diners.
instructions: Peel and cube sweet potatoes into 1-inch pieces.
In a saucepan, combine sweet potatoes, water, Shaoxing wine, and a pinch of salt, bring to a boil and simmer until tender, about 12 minutes.
While sweet potatoes cook, season scallops with a splash of soy sauce and set aside.
In a wok, heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil over high heat; add scallops and sear for 2 minutes, then remove and set aside.
In the same wok, add 1 tbsp sesame oil, ginger, and garlic; stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add braised sweet potatoes to the wok, stir to combine.
Return scallops to the wok, drizzle with remaining soy sauce and a teaspoon of white mustard powder, and toss gently to coat.
Stir in a splash of water to create a light sauce, and cook for another 2 minutes, letting flavors meld.
Finish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds, give one final stir.
Serve hot over steamed rice or alone as a comforting dish.
